behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction describes the encoded patterns (%5BparamName%5D, %5B...paramName%5D, %5B%5B...paramName%5D%5D) and malformed cases (%5Bparam, %5, 100%25complete) that tests check; AGENTS.md rules are referenced.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes expected behavior (decode before matching) without specifying the helper function name or exact code changes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on dynamicSegments param names/types via parseAppRoute execution, not on specific helper names or line positions; alternative implementations would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute parseAppRoute via tsx subprocess and inspect JSON output of dynamicSegments.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each f2p asserts specific param names like 'projectSlug', 'teamSlug' appear in dynamicSegments which requires actual decoding logic.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover single/multiple/mixed dynamic, catchall, optional catchall, malformed encodings, and AGENTS.md rules, matching instruction requirements.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived, Repo CI/CD) and descriptive names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code fix, not a data-producing task.

no_typos.reason: Paths, filenames, and identifiers are consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the bug domain (route parser) without naming the specific file/line to change.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, no config edits involved.
